purpose: Model catalog for selecting the HuggingFace checkpoint text_server.py loads as the LLM game master. All entries are non-thinking, ungated instruct models (no <think> leakage, no HF license wall) with reliable JSON output, run locally via transformers on the installed PyTorch wheel.

nvidia.note: Returns one line per GPU; take the max. Divide by 1024 for GiB.

amd.note: Parse 'VRAM Total Memory (B)' column; bytes. bitsandbytes NF4 is CUDA-only: on ROCm use a bf16 entry that fits instead.

apple_silicon.note: Unified memory in bytes; treat ~65% of total RAM as usable. NF4 unavailable: use bf16 entries on the mps device.

choices.label: Entry GPU, 4 GB or less (GTX 1650, laptop iGPU)

selection_rules.strategy: Pick the highest-quality_rank model whose min_vram_gb <= detected VRAM; ties broken by smaller weights_vram_gb. Launch text_server.py --model <hf_id> (add --no-4bit only when quant is bf16). If VRAM is within 1 GB of min_vram_gb, halve --ctx instead of downgrading the model.
selection_rules.headroom_note: min_vram_gb already includes ~2 GB headroom for the KV cache at the game's ~6k-token context, plus ~2.5 GB for the SD-Turbo image model sharing the card; add serve.py --no-image to reclaim that.
selection_rules.download_note: NF4 entries quantize at load from the published bf16 weights, so the first-run download is the full bf16 size (download_gb), not weights_vram_gb.
selection_rules.context_floor: Reject any model whose native_context < game_requirement minimum ctx (8192). All current entries pass; the rule guards future additions.

models.notes: Absolute floor and the only entry usable on CPU (fp32, expect ~1 min/turn). Expect JSON slips and state drift; fine as a demo/degenerate baseline.
Fastest GPU option; no bitsandbytes needed. Small models track long histories poorly; rely on the harness's rolling history window.
Strong structured-output family without Qwen3's thinking mode; coexists with SD-Turbo on a 16 GB card with room to spare.

The default (llm_client.DEFAULT_MODEL). 8B total / ~4.5B effective parameters via per-layer embeddings; multimodal checkpoint but the game uses text only. Ungated on HF (unlike older gemma repos). Loads through AutoModelForCausalLM on transformers >= 5.14. If NF4 quantization misbehaves with the per-layer embeddings, fall back to --no-4bit (bf16, ~16 GB VRAM, pair with serve.py --no-image).
14B-class quality in under 10 GB of VRAM. Best pick for a 16 GB card if the slower tokens/sec is acceptable.
For 24 GB+ cards only. Quality ceiling for a non-thinking local game master.

excluded_families.reason: mandatory <think> reasoning output breaks JSON parsing
usable only with thinking explicitly disabled; excluded from auto-pick for safety
license-gated on HuggingFace -- would force every player through an account + access request before first launch. gemma-4 repos are ungated (verified anonymously) and allowed.

runtime.context_note: text_server.py --ctx (default 16384) bounds prompt + completion tokens and therefore the KV cache. The game history reaches ~6-8k tokens by turn 20; keep --ctx >= 8192 or early decisions (and party facts) silently fall out of context.
runtime.kv_cache_note: min_vram_gb includes model weights + KV cache at recommended_ctx + SD-Turbo + ~1 GB runtime overhead. KV cache grows linearly with --ctx; halve it to reclaim roughly half.
